segments.text: Then Job answered and said:
Oh, that my grief were fully weighed, And my calamity laid with it on the scales!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For then it would be heavier than the sand of the sea— Therefore my words have been rash.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me; My spirit drinks in their poison; The terrors of God are arrayed against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Does the wild donkey bray when it has grass, Or does the ox low over its fodder?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Can flavorless food be eaten without salt? Or is there any taste in the white of an egg?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My soul refuses to touch them; They are as loathsome food to me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Oh, that I might have my request, That God would grant me the thing that I long for!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: That it would please God to crush me, That He would loose His hand and cut me off!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then I would still have comfort; Though in anguish I would exult, He will not spare; For I have not concealed the words of the Holy One.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: What strength do I have, that I should hope? And what is my end, that I should prolong my life?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Is my strength the strength of stones? Or is my flesh bronze?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Is my help not within me? And is success driven from me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: To him who is afflicted, kindness should be shown by his friend, Even though he forsakes the fear of the Almighty.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My brothers have dealt deceitfully like a brook, Like the streams of the brooks that pass away,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Which are dark because of the ice, And into which the snow vanishes.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When it is warm, they cease to flow; When it is hot, they vanish from their place.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The paths of their way turn aside, They go nowhere and perish.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The caravans of Tema look, The travelers of Sheba hope for them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They are disappointed because they were confident; They come there and are confused.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For now you are nothing, You see terror and are afraid.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Did I ever say, 'Bring something to me'? Or, 'Offer a bribe for me from your wealth'?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Or, 'Deliver me from the enemy's hand'? Or, 'Redeem me from the hand of oppressors'?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Teach me, and I will hold my tongue; Cause me to understand wherein I have erred.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How forceful are right words! But what does your arguing prove?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do you intend to rebuke my words, And the speeches of a desperate one, which are as wind?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yes, you overwhelm the fatherless, And you undermine your friend.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Now therefore, be pleased to look at me; For I would never lie to your face.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yield now, let there be no injustice! Yes, concede, my righteousness still stands!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Is there injustice on my tongue? Cannot my taste discern the unsavory?
segments.speaker: Job
